answer.notes: All 151 entities are identical 5-vertex closed LWPOLYLINEs on the INSUL (단열재/insulation) sublayer of the floor-plan layer; each is a small closed rectangular hatch tile, not an open wall-boundary line, so no entity is listed as a wall line. bbox is not derivable (no LINE entities). The tile count scales with the length of wall being insulated, so the definition is spatially tied to walls without containing wall centerline/boundary geometry itself — classified as a repeated material symbol, low wall_likelihood.

answer.notes: 23-entity INSERT+LINE+WIPEOUT bundle nests an INSERT of block 'X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대' (flagpole hoist-stand), so semantically this is a site fixture symbol, not a building wall, despite a long (~3320u) thin bbox. Several LINE pairs run parallel ~50 units apart across the full length, a wall-pair-like geometric signature, which is why wall_likelihood is not zero; but the block-name evidence (flagpole mount) dominates the role classification over the raw geometry.
